headline: Amazon Rainforest
description: The Amazon Rainforest (5.5 million km², 9 countries) is the world's largest tropical rainforest, producing 20% of Earth's oxygen. Biodiversity: 10% of known species — 40,000 plant species, 1,300 birds, 3,000 fish, 427 mammals. Indigenous peoples have lived there for 11,000+ years.
